metadata.title: Docker Security Hardening + MLSecOps — H3+H4 Implementation

setup.step: Verify branch exists (was created at session start)

phase.name: Critical Safety
phase.description: Immediate security fixes that must be done before any training run. Does NOT touch Dockerfiles (those are Phase 2). Outputs: comprehensive .dockerignore, pre-commit hadolint hook, OPTUNA credential fix, MODEL_CACHE_HOST_PATH fix.

task.name: Verify NVIDIA Container Toolkit version
task.description: CVE-2025-23266 (CVSS 9.0 Critical, NVIDIAScape) — container-to-host escape. Fixed in Container Toolkit v1.17.8+. Must verify before any GPU training.
task.test-first: Write tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_nvidia_ctk_version.py: - test_nvidia_ctk_version_is_safe: parse `nvidia-ctk --version` output, assert version >= (1, 17, 8). Skip if nvidia-ctk not installed. Use subprocess.run, parse with str.split() (not regex — Rule #16). - test_cuda_compat_hook_not_disabled_by_config: Read /etc/nvidia-container-toolkit/config.toml if it exists; warn if disable-cuda-compat-lib-hook is True (override to fix, not workaround).
task.acceptance: nvidia-ctk --version output parses to >= 1.17.8. Test passes or provides clear remediation instructions.
task.on-failure: If nvidia-ctk is below 1.17.8: STOP. Document in a GitHub issue. Do NOT proceed with training until patched. Patch: sudo apt-get install --only-upgrade nvidia-container-toolkit

task.name: Add comprehensive .dockerignore
task.description: Current .dockerignore is empty/minimal. A missing .env in build context can leak credentials into image layers. This also reduces build context size.
task.test-first: Write tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_dockerignore.py: - test_dockerignore_exists: assert Path(".dockerignore").exists() - test_dockerignore_excludes_env: read .dockerignore, assert ".env" in lines - test_dockerignore_excludes_git: assert ".git" in lines - test_dockerignore_excludes_mlruns: assert "mlruns/" in lines - test_dockerignore_excludes_pycache: assert "__pycache__/" in lines - test_dockerignore_excludes_pytest_cache: assert ".pytest_cache/" in lines Parse with Path.read_text().splitlines() — not regex.
task.implementation: Write .dockerignore at repo root with: .env, *.env, secrets/, .git, .gitignore, __pycache__/, *.pyc, *.egg-info/, .pytest_cache/, .mypy_cache/, .ruff_cache/, dist/, build/, mlruns/, outputs/duckdb/, data/minivess/, data/raw/, outputs/checkpoints/, *.onnx, node_modules/, .vscode/, .idea/, *.swp, *.tmp, *.log, docs/, wiki/, .github/, tests/

task.name: Fix OPTUNA_STORAGE_URL credential hardcode in compose
task.description: docker-compose.flows.yml line 34 has: OPTUNA_STORAGE_URL: ${OPTUNA_STORAGE_URL:-postgresql+psycopg2://minivess:minivess_secret@postgres:5432/optuna} The fallback hardcodes "minivess_secret". This violates Rule #22. Fix: use ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD} in the fallback, not a literal password.
task.test-first: Write tests/v2/unit/test_env_single_source.py (add to existing or create): - test_no_hardcoded_password_in_compose_flows: read deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml with yaml.safe_load(), find OPTUNA_STORAGE_URL value, assert "minivess_secret" not in the literal string (should be ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD} reference). Parse with yaml.safe_load() — not regex (Rule #16).
task.acceptance: Test passes. yaml.safe_load confirms no literal "minivess_secret" in compose value.

task.name: Fix MODEL_CACHE_HOST_PATH hardcoded home directory
task.description: docker-compose.flows.yml has fallback /home/petteri/download_cache. This breaks silently on any other machine. Remove fallback; require MODEL_CACHE_HOST_PATH to be set explicitly in .env.
task.test-first: Add to tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_compose_correctness.py (create if missing): - test_model_cache_has_no_hardcoded_home_path: read compose with yaml.safe_load(), find model_cache volume definition, assert "/home/petteri" not in any value.
task.implementation: In deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml, change train and hpo services: - ${MODEL_CACHE_HOST_PATH:-/home/petteri/download_cache}:/model_cache To: - ${MODEL_CACHE_HOST_PATH}:/model_cache Update .env.example: add comment "# Required: no default. Set to your local model cache path." Update deployment/CLAUDE.md: document that MODEL_CACHE_HOST_PATH must be set.
task.acceptance: Test passes. No /home/petteri in compose files.

task.name: Add Hadolint to pre-commit configuration
task.description: Hadolint is the standard Dockerfile linter. Catches: missing HEALTHCHECK, missing --no-install-recommends, ADD instead of COPY, root USER, etc. Add as a pre-commit hook to enforce Dockerfile quality continuously.
task.test-first: Write tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_hadolint_config.py: - test_hadolint_config_exists: assert Path(".hadolint.yaml").exists() - test_pre_commit_includes_hadolint: read .pre-commit-config.yaml with yaml.safe_load(), find hadolint entry in repos list, assert it exists. - test_dockerfiles_pass_hadolint: skip if hadolint not installed. Run hadolint via subprocess on each Dockerfile in deployment/docker/. Assert exit code 0 for all. Log any warnings.
task.implementation:
1. Create .hadolint.yaml at repo root: failure-threshold: error ignored: [DL3008] # apt version pinning — Ubuntu LTS is stable override: error: [DL3002, DL3006, DL4006]
2. Add to .pre-commit-config.yaml: - repo: https://github.com/hadolint/hadolint rev: v2.12.0 hooks: - id: hadolint args: [--config, .hadolint.yaml]
task.acceptance: hadolint runs cleanly on all Dockerfiles in deployment/docker/.

task.name: Phase 1 commit
task.steps: 1. uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/deployment/ -x -q 2. uv run ruff check . and uv run mypy src/ 3. git add .dockerignore .hadolint.yaml .pre-commit-config.yaml deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml .env.example deployment/CLAUDE.md tests/v2/unit/deployment/ 4. git commit -m "fix(docker): Phase 1 critical safety — dockerignore, hadolint, credential fixes"

phase.name: Dockerfile.base Refactoring — H3+H4
phase.description: Convert Dockerfile.base from single-stage to two-stage: builder: nvidia/cuda:12.6.3-devel-ubuntu24.04 (uv, git, curl, CUDA headers) runner: nvidia/cuda:12.6.3-runtime-ubuntu24.04 (Python only, no build tools) Add BuildKit cache mounts for uv (critical for rebuild speed). Add OCI labels, PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE, HEALTHCHECK, proper umask via entrypoint. Fix: uv.lock* glob → uv.lock; venv permissions; uv --frozen flag.

task.name: Write Dockerfile.base tests (RED phase)
task.description: Before changing Dockerfile.base, write tests that verify the properties we want. These will fail until the Dockerfile is updated.
task.test-first:
Write tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_dockerfile_base.py:
- test_dockerfile_base_uses_two_stages: read Dockerfile.base, parse with [line for line in content.splitlines() if line.strip().startswith("FROM")]. Assert len(from_lines) >= 2 (builder + runner stages). Assert "AS builder" in from_lines[0]. Assert "AS runner" in from_lines[1] (or check for the runner FROM).
- test_dockerfile_base_builder_uses_devel: the builder FROM must reference "devel" in the image name (cuda:*-devel-*).
- test_dockerfile_base_runner_uses_runtime: the runner FROM must reference "runtime" and NOT "devel".
- test_dockerfile_base_no_git_in_runner: the runner stage (content after second FROM) must not contain "apt-get install" with "git". Use ast-free line parsing: split on FROM boundaries, check second stage.
- test_dockerfile_base_no_uv_in_runner: runner stage must not COPY uv binary (no "COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv" in runner stage).
- test_dockerfile_base_no_pip_in_runner: runner stage must not install python3-pip.
- test_dockerfile_base_has_oci_labels: content must contain "org.opencontainers.image.source" and "org.opencontainers.image.revision".
- test_dockerfile_base_has_buildkit_cache_mount: content must contain "--mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/uv".
- test_dockerfile_base_uv_sync_frozen: content must contain "--frozen" in uv sync command.
- test_dockerfile_base_pythondontwritebytecode: content must contain "PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE".
- test_dockerfile_base_uv_lock_exact: content must contain "uv.lock" as a standalone token (not "uv.lock*" glob).
- test_dockerfile_base_venv_group_only: the chmod command must not use "a+rX" — use "g+rX" (group-readable, not world-readable).
- test_dockerfile_base_has_uv_env_vars: content must contain all three: "UV_COMPILE_BYTECODE", "UV_LINK_MODE", "UV_PYTHON_DOWNLOADS".
All file reads use Path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"). No regex (Rule #16). Use str methods: splitlines(), partition(), split(), "in" operator.
task.acceptance: All 13 tests FAIL (expected in RED phase — Dockerfile not yet changed).

task.name: Implement new Dockerfile.base (GREEN phase)
task.description: Rewrite deployment/docker/Dockerfile.base with multi-stage H3+H4 pattern. Preserve the 3-layer hierarchy.
task.implementation:
Write deployment/docker/Dockerfile.base:
``` # syntax=docker/dockerfile:1 # MinIVess MLOps v2 — Base Docker image (Layer 2) # H3+H4: Multi-stage build — builder uses devel, runner uses runtime. # Build tools (uv, git, curl) are confined to builder stage only. # # Build: # docker build \ # --build-arg GIT_COMMIT=$(git rev-parse HEAD) \ # --build-arg BUILD_DATE=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") \ # -t minivess-base:latest \ # -f deployment/docker/Dockerfile.base .
# ── Stage 1: Builder ───────────────────────────────────────────────── # Uses devel variant for CUDA headers + nvcc (needed by some MONAI ops). # Contains uv, git, curl — NEVER in final image. FROM nvidia/cuda:12.6.3-devel-ubuntu24.04 AS builder
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \ python3 \ python3-venv \ git \ curl \ && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
# Install uv — BUILD STAGE ONLY COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:latest /uv /usr/local/bin/uv
# uv env: compile bytecodes, copy-link mode (required for cache mounts), # never auto-download Python, never install dev deps. ENV UV_COMPILE_BYTECODE=1 \ UV_LINK_MODE=copy \ UV_PYTHON_DOWNLOADS=never \ UV_NO_DEV=1
WORKDIR /app
# Phase A: install deps only (cacheable when only src/ changes) # --mount=type=cache: persists uv download cache across rebuilds locally. # --frozen: treats uv.lock as immutable (fail if out of sync with pyproject.toml). RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/uv \ --mount=type=bind,source=uv.lock,target=uv.lock \ --mount=type=bind,source=pyproject.toml,target=pyproject.toml \ uv sync --frozen --no-dev --no-install-project
# Phase B: copy source and install project COPY src/ src/ COPY configs/ configs/ RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/uv \ uv sync --frozen --no-dev
# ── Stage 2: Runner ────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Minimal runtime — NO uv, NO git, NO curl, NO pip, NO CUDA headers. # PyTorch wheels bundle their own CUDA math libs (cublas, cufft, etc.) # so nvidia/cuda:*-runtime is sufficient. FROM nvidia/cuda:12.6.3-runtime-ubuntu24.04 AS runner
# Build-time provenance args (inject via --build-arg at CI time) ARG GIT_COMMIT=unknown ARG BUILD_DATE=unknown ARG UID=1000 ARG GID=1000
# OCI standard image annotations (NIST SP 800-190 traceability) LABEL maintainer="petteri.teikari@gmail.com" LABEL project="minivess-mlops" LABEL org.opencontainers.image.title="minivess-base" LABEL org.opencontainers.image.source="https://github.com/minivess-mlops/minivess-mlops" LABEL org.opencontainers.image.revision="${GIT_COMMIT}" LABEL org.opencontainers.image.created="${BUILD_DATE}" LABEL org.opencontainers.image.licenses="CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0" LABEL org.opencontainers.image.base.name="nvidia/cuda:12.6.3-runtime-ubuntu24.04"
# Minimal runtime Python only — no pip, no build tools RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \ python3 \ python3-venv \ && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
# Non-root user with configurable UID/GID (matches host user for bind mounts) RUN groupadd -g $GID minivess 2>/dev/null || true && \ useradd --create-home --shell /bin/bash -u $UID -g $GID -o minivess
WORKDIR /app
# Copy only the built venv and application code from builder # NEVER copy: uv binary, git history, build caches, devel headers COPY --from=builder --chown=minivess:minivess /app/.venv /app/.venv COPY --from=builder --chown=minivess:minivess /app/src /app/src COPY --from=builder --chown=minivess:minivess /app/configs /app/configs
# Group-readable venv (not world-readable — limit to minivess group) RUN chmod -R g+rX /app/.venv
USER minivess
# Python environment ENV PATH=/app/.venv/bin:$PATH ENV PYTHONPATH=/app/src ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1
# HEALTHCHECK: verify Python and core package imports work HEALTHCHECK --interval=60s --timeout=10s --retries=3 --start-period=120s \ CMD python3 -c "import sys; sys.exit(0)" || exit 1 ```
task.acceptance: All 13 tests from T-02.1 now PASS (GREEN phase).
task.on-failure: If docker build fails: check uv.lock exists at repo root. If cache mount causes issues: add --no-cache flag for first build. If CUDA devel image unavailable: use runtime for both stages (less optimal).

task.name: Create Dockerfile.mlflow with baked-in deps
task.description: Currently docker-compose.yml installs psycopg2-binary and boto3 via pip at every MLflow startup (15-30 seconds, non-reproducible, breaks air-gapped). Create a custom MLflow Dockerfile with deps baked in.
task.test-first: Add to tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_dockerfile_mlflow.py: - test_dockerfile_mlflow_exists: assert Path("deployment/docker/Dockerfile.mlflow").exists() - test_mlflow_dockerfile_has_pinned_psycopg2: read file, assert "psycopg2-binary" in content - test_mlflow_dockerfile_has_boto3: assert "boto3" in content - test_mlflow_dockerfile_no_pip_at_runtime: compose entrypoint for mlflow must NOT contain "pip install" (use yaml.safe_load to verify compose). - test_mlflow_image_reference_in_compose: mlflow service must use "build:" referencing Dockerfile.mlflow, not just "image: ghcr.io/mlflow/mlflow:*"
task.implementation:
Create deployment/docker/Dockerfile.mlflow: ``` FROM ghcr.io/mlflow/mlflow:v3.10.0 # Bake in PostgreSQL + S3/MinIO drivers at build time. # These are stable deps — pin to specific versions for reproducibility. RUN pip install --no-cache-dir \ psycopg2-binary==2.9.10 \ boto3==1.34.162 ```
Update deployment/docker-compose.yml mlflow service: - Change image: to build: context + dockerfile: Dockerfile.mlflow - Remove pip install from entrypoint (keep only mlflow server command) - Update image: to minivess-mlflow:v3.10.0
task.acceptance: MLflow service starts without pip install step. All 5 tests pass.

task.name: Add OCI labels to per-flow Dockerfiles
task.description: Each flow Dockerfile (train, data, analyze, etc.) should add flow-specific OCI labels for traceability. LABEL inherits from base; add flow-specific ones.
task.test-first: Add to tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_dockerfile_flows.py: - test_all_flow_dockerfiles_have_flow_label: for each Dockerfile.{flow} in deployment/docker/, verify LABEL flow= is present. - test_all_flow_dockerfiles_have_description_label: verify LABEL description= is present. - test_no_flow_dockerfile_runs_apt: verify no flow Dockerfile contains "apt-get" (all deps belong in base). - test_no_flow_dockerfile_runs_uv: verify no flow Dockerfile contains "uv sync". - test_all_flow_dockerfiles_inherit_from_base: verify FROM line is "FROM minivess-base:latest" (not FROM nvidia/cuda directly).
task.implementation:
For each per-flow Dockerfile, ensure these labels are present: LABEL flow="{name}" LABEL description="{description}" LABEL org.opencontainers.image.title="minivess-{flow}"
No other changes to per-flow Dockerfiles in this task.

task.name: Phase 2 commit
task.steps: 1. uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/deployment/ -x -q (all pass) 2. Run hadolint on Dockerfile.base and Dockerfile.mlflow 3. git add deployment/docker/Dockerfile.base deployment/docker/Dockerfile.mlflow deployment/docker/Dockerfile.* deployment/docker-compose.yml tests/v2/unit/deployment/ 4. git commit -m "feat(docker): H4 multi-stage Dockerfile.base + MLflow custom image"

phase.name: Docker Compose Hardening
phase.description: Add to docker-compose.flows.yml: - cap_drop: ALL + security_opt: no-new-privileges (CIS 5.3, 5.25) - Resource limits: mem_limit, cpus, pids_limit per flow (CIS 5.11, OWASP D07) - shm_size: 8g for train and hpo (CRITICAL: MONAI DataLoader Bus error prevention) - Log driver: local, non-blocking, rotation (production logging) - restart: "no" explicitly on flow containers (prevent ghost runs) - minio-init service for bucket auto-creation (fixes #528 pattern) - volume-init service for ownership setup (fixes #529 pattern) Fix docker-compose.yml: - Pin MinIO image tag - Remove icc: false from daemon.json recommendation (breaks inter-container comm) - Restrict port bindings to 127.0.0.1 (localhost-only)

task.name: Write compose hardening tests (RED phase)
task.test-first:
Write tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_compose_hardening.py:
All parsing via yaml.safe_load(Path(...).read_text(encoding="utf-8")). No regex (Rule #16).
For docker-compose.flows.yml:
- test_all_flows_have_cap_drop_all: for each service in flows compose, assert service["cap_drop"] == ["ALL"] or service.get("cap_drop", []) == ["ALL"].
- test_all_flows_have_no_new_privileges: for each service, assert "no-new-privileges:true" in service.get("security_opt", []).
- test_train_has_shm_size: train service must have shm_size set. Assert shm_size value is not None. (Can be string "8g" or int.)
- test_hpo_has_shm_size: hpo service must have shm_size.
- test_train_has_mem_limit: assert "mem_limit" in train service config.
- test_all_flows_have_pids_limit: for each service, assert pids_limit > 0.
- test_all_flows_have_log_driver: for each service, assert service.get("logging", {}).get("driver") is not None.
- test_all_flows_have_restart_no: for each service, assert service.get("restart") == "no".
- test_minio_init_service_exists: docker-compose.yml must have "minio-init" service (or "minivess-minio-init" container_name).
- test_volume_init_documented: deployment/CLAUDE.md must contain "make init-volumes" or "volume-init" (init step is documented).
- test_no_hardcoded_home_in_model_cache: no "/home/petteri" in any compose value. (Covered by Phase 1 T-01.4 as well.)
For docker-compose.yml: - test_minio_image_pinned: minio service image must NOT be "minio/minio:latest". It must contain a specific version or date tag.
- test_ports_bound_to_localhost: each port mapping in docker-compose.yml (postgres, minio, mlflow, prefect) should use "127.0.0.1:" prefix. Note: This may be a warning, not a hard failure, since some dev setups need 0.0.0.0. Mark as @pytest.mark.security_audit.
task.acceptance: All tests FAIL (RED — compose not yet updated).

task.name: Add capability drop and security options to flows compose
task.description: Add to ALL services in docker-compose.flows.yml: cap_drop: [ALL] security_opt: [no-new-privileges:true] restart: "no" Use x-common-security anchor to avoid duplication.
task.implementation:
Add to docker-compose.flows.yml after x-common-env anchor:
x-common-security: &common-security cap_drop: - ALL security_opt: - no-new-privileges:true restart: "no"
Then in each service, add: <<: *common-security
NOTE: GPU flows (train, hpo) do NOT need cap_add after dropping ALL. PyTorch/CUDA/Optuna/Prefect require zero Linux capabilities. EXCEPTION: If MONAI pin_memory DataLoader fails with mlock errors, add cap_add: [IPC_LOCK] to train and hpo ONLY and document why.

task.name: Add resource limits and shm_size
task.description: CRITICAL: shm_size for train and hpo prevents MONAI 3D DataLoader Bus error. Formula: num_workers * batch_size * sample_bytes * 2 For 3D MiniVess patches (256x256x128, float32): 8GB covers any realistic workload. Also add mem_limit, cpus, pids_limit per flow class.
task.implementation:
Add x-resource-* anchors to docker-compose.flows.yml:
x-gpu-resources: &gpu-resources mem_limit: 32g memswap_limit: 32g cpus: 8.0 pids_limit: 2048 shm_size: "8g" # CRITICAL: prevents MONAI DataLoader Bus error
x-cpu-resources: &cpu-resources mem_limit: 16g memswap_limit: 16g cpus: 4.0 pids_limit: 512
x-light-resources: &light-resources mem_limit: 4g cpus: 2.0 pids_limit: 256
Apply per service: - train, hpo: *gpu-resources - data, analyze, post_training, acquisition, biostatistics: *cpu-resources - dashboard, qa, pipeline, annotation: *light-resources
IMPORTANT: For docker compose run (not up), shm_size from compose is NOT automatically applied. Scripts using `docker compose run` MUST add: --shm-size 8g (for train/hpo) Update scripts/run_debug.sh and deployment/CLAUDE.md with this requirement.
task.acceptance: shm_size tests pass for train and hpo. mem_limit and pids_limit tests pass for all services. run_debug.sh includes --shm-size for GPU flows.

task.name: Add log driver configuration
task.description: Default docker logging (json-file without rotation) produces unbounded log files during long training runs. The local driver is more efficient than json-file. Non-blocking mode prevents log backend slowness from blocking training process.
task.implementation:
Add to docker-compose.flows.yml after x-common-security:
x-logging: &default-logging logging: driver: local options: max-size: "50m" max-file: "10" compress: "true" mode: non-blocking max-buffer-size: "4m"
Apply to ALL services: <<: *default-logging
Note: local driver still supports `docker logs` command. It uses binary format (more efficient than json-file) with auto-compression. 500MB max per container (50MB × 10 files) is appropriate for training.
task.acceptance: Log driver test passes for all services.

task.name: Add minio-init service for bucket auto-creation
task.description: Issue #528: MinIO does not auto-create buckets. The mlflow-artifacts bucket must exist before MLflow can log artifacts. Add an init container.
task.implementation:
Add to deployment/docker-compose.yml (in dev/monitoring/full profiles):
minio-init: image: minio/mc:RELEASE.2025-02-08T19-54-51Z container_name: minivess-minio-init profiles: ["dev", "monitoring", "full"] depends_on: minio: condition: service_healthy entrypoint: > /bin/sh -c " mc alias set local http://minivess-minio:9000 $${MINIO_ROOT_USER} $${MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD} && mc mb --ignore-existing local/mlflow-artifacts && mc mb --ignore-existing local/model-registry && echo 'MinIO buckets initialized successfully' " environment: MINIO_ROOT_USER: ${MINIO_ROOT_USER:-minioadmin} MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD: ${MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD:-minioadmin_secret} networks: - minivess restart: "no" labels: project: minivess-mlops managed-by: docker-compose
task.acceptance: minio-init service exists in compose. Test passes.

task.name: Pin MinIO image tag and add volume-init documentation
task.description: minio/minio:latest is non-reproducible. Pin to a specific release. Document volume initialization as a `make init-volumes` target.
task.implementation:
1. In docker-compose.yml, change: image: minio/minio:latest To: image: minio/minio:RELEASE.2025-02-08T19-54-51Z (Use a recent stable release — verify at hub.docker.com/r/minio/minio/tags)
2. Add Makefile target (create Makefile if not exists): init-volumes: @echo "Initializing Docker named volumes..." docker run --rm --user root \ -v deployment_checkpoint_cache:/app/checkpoints \ -v deployment_logs_data:/app/logs \ -v deployment_mlruns_data:/app/mlruns \ -v deployment_data_cache:/app/data \ -v deployment_configs_splits:/app/configs/splits \ ubuntu:22.04 \ chown -R 1000:1000 \ /app/checkpoints /app/logs /app/mlruns /app/data /app/configs/splits @echo "Volume ownership initialized"
scan: @echo "Scanning MinIVess Docker images with Trivy..." @which trivy || (echo "Install: curl -sfL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aquasecurity/trivy/main/contrib/install.sh | sh -s -- -b /usr/local/bin" && exit 1) @for flow in base train data analyze deploy dashboard qa hpo post_training; do \ echo "Scanning minivess-$$flow:latest..."; \ trivy image --exit-code 0 --severity CRITICAL,HIGH --ignore-unfixed \ minivess-$$flow:latest 2>/dev/null || echo "Image not built yet: minivess-$$flow"; \ done
3. Update deployment/CLAUDE.md: add "make init-volumes" to setup steps.
task.acceptance: MinIO image tag is not "latest". Makefile has init-volumes and scan targets.

task.name: Phase 3 commit
task.steps: 1. uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/deployment/ -x -q 2. git add deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml deployment/docker-compose.yml Makefile deployment/CLAUDE.md scripts/run_debug.sh tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_compose_hardening.py 3. git commit -m "feat(docker): Phase 3 compose hardening — cap_drop, shm_size, resource limits, minio-init"

phase.name: MLSecOps Tooling
phase.description: Integrate Trivy scanning, verify NVIDIA CTK version, add seccomp audit scaffolding, update deployment/CLAUDE.md with production runbook. This phase produces: Trivy CI gate, seccomp audit profiles directory, SBOM generation target, updated operational docs.

task.name: Add Trivy scan integration
task.description: Trivy is the recommended scanner for ML/AI container images. Policy: block on CRITICAL CVEs only initially (NVIDIA base images carry many HIGH CVEs with no fix available — use --ignore-unfixed). The scan Makefile target was added in T-03.6; expand it here.
task.test-first: Add to tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_security_tooling.py: - test_makefile_has_scan_target: read Makefile, assert "scan:" in content. - test_makefile_has_sbom_target: assert "sbom:" in content. - test_trivy_policy_uses_ignore_unfixed: assert "--ignore-unfixed" in scan target. - test_trivy_policy_blocks_on_critical: assert "--severity CRITICAL" in scan target.
task.acceptance: All 4 tests pass. `make scan` runs without error (even if images not built).

task.name: Create seccomp profile scaffolding
task.description: Seccomp profiles restrict syscalls available to containers (CIS 5.21, OWASP D05). Phase 4 creates the directory structure and audit profile. Actual per-flow profiles are generated by running each flow with the audit profile and observing syscalls — this is done as operational work after deploy.
task.test-first: Add to tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_security_tooling.py: - test_seccomp_profiles_dir_exists: assert Path("deployment/seccomp/").exists() - test_seccomp_audit_profile_exists: assert Path("deployment/seccomp/audit.json").exists() - test_seccomp_audit_profile_is_valid_json: json.loads(audit_profile_content) must not raise. Use json.loads() — not yaml or regex. - test_seccomp_audit_profile_action_is_log: parsed["defaultAction"] == "SCMP_ACT_LOG" - test_seccomp_readme_exists: assert Path("deployment/seccomp/README.md").exists()
task.implementation:
Create deployment/seccomp/ directory.
Create deployment/seccomp/audit.json: { "defaultAction": "SCMP_ACT_LOG", "architectures": ["SCMP_ARCH_X86_64"], "syscalls": [] } Note: SCMP_ACT_LOG logs all syscalls without blocking — use in staging to discover required syscalls before building allowlist profile.
Create deployment/seccomp/README.md documenting: 1. How to run with audit profile (docker compose run --security-opt seccomp=...) 2. How to extract syscalls from /var/log/audit/audit.log 3. How to build per-flow allowlist profile 4. Warning: seccomp is silently ignored with privileged: true 5. IPC_LOCK caveat: MONAI pin_memory may need cap_add: [IPC_LOCK]
Add to Makefile: seccomp-audit-train: @echo "Running train flow with seccomp audit profile..." @docker compose -f deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml run --rm -T \ --security-opt seccomp=deployment/seccomp/audit.json \ --shm-size 8g \ train </dev/null 2>&1 | head -50 @echo "Check /var/log/audit/audit.log for syscall log"
task.acceptance: All 5 seccomp tests pass. audit.json is valid JSON.

task.name: Update deployment/CLAUDE.md production runbook
task.description: Update deployment/CLAUDE.md with all new patterns from this plan: - Multi-stage build instructions - shm_size requirement for docker compose run - CVE-2025-23266 mitigation (verify nvidia-ctk version) - Trivy scan before deployment - minio-init and volume-init setup steps - Seccomp audit workflow
task.test-first: Add to tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_deployment_docs.py: - test_claude_md_documents_shm_size: read deployment/CLAUDE.md, assert "--shm-size" in content. - test_claude_md_documents_nvidia_ctk: assert "nvidia-ctk" in content. - test_claude_md_documents_trivy: assert "trivy" in content.lower() - test_claude_md_documents_minio_init: assert "minio-init" in content or "make init-buckets" in content. - test_claude_md_documents_multi_stage: assert "builder" in content and "runner" in content.
task.implementation: Add new sections to deployment/CLAUDE.md: - "First-time Setup" (ordered: network → init-volumes → start infra → minio-init → scan → run flow) - "CVE-2025-23266 Verification" (nvidia-ctk --version check) - "shm_size Requirement" (why, formula, how to pass to docker compose run) - "Image Scanning" (make scan, make sbom) - "Seccomp Profiles" (link to deployment/seccomp/README.md)

task.name: Final verification and PR
task.steps: 1. Run full test suite: uv run pytest tests/ -x -q --ignore=tests/e2e 2. Run lint: uv run ruff check src/ tests/ deployment/ 3. Run type check: uv run mypy src/ 4. Run hadolint on all Dockerfiles: hadolint deployment/docker/Dockerfile.* 5. Build minivess-base locally to verify multi-stage works: docker build --build-arg GIT_COMMIT=$(git rev-parse HEAD) \ --build-arg BUILD_DATE=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") \ -t minivess-base:latest \ -f deployment/docker/Dockerfile.base . 6. Run make scan against built image 7. git add -A and git commit -m "feat(docker): Phase 4 MLSecOps tooling" 8. git push -u origin feat/docker-hardening 9. gh pr create --title "feat(docker): H3+H4 hardening — multi-stage build, cap_drop, shm_size, MLSecOps" --body "Implements docker-security-hardening-mlsecops-plan.xml. Multi-stage Dockerfile.base, compose hardening, Trivy integration. Related: #537 (multi-tier optimization — tracked separately)"

verification.acceptance: Zero failures. Zero skips except nvidia-ctk tests on CPU-only CI runners.

future-work.item: Multi-tier base images (GPU/CPU/web/light) — tracked in GitHub issue #537
Per-flow seccomp allowlist profiles (requires running each flow with audit profile first)
read_only: true for non-GPU flows (requires CUDA tmpfs discovery — ~/.nv, /tmp/cuda)
Chainguard CUDA/PyTorch images (when commercial budget available)
SLSA Build Level 2 attestation (requires registry push infrastructure)
cosign image signing (requires cosign key management decision)
Per-flow MinIO IAM policies (scoped bucket access, not root credentials)
HashiCorp Vault for secret management (replaces env var credentials)
AppArmor profile for GPU flows (ubuntu 24.04 LSM — document docker-default adequacy)

guardrails.rule: NEVER use import re or regex for parsing Dockerfile, YAML, or JSON — Rule #16
ALL config values come from .env.example first — Rule #22
TDD mandatory: write failing tests BEFORE implementing — Rule #2
NO standalone script shortcuts: training goes through docker compose run — Rule #17
GitHub Actions CI is DISABLED: all validation runs locally — Rule #21
The 3-layer Docker hierarchy must be preserved (only Layer 1/2 of base changes)
cap_drop: ALL is added to ALL flow services — no exceptions without documented reason
shm_size: "8g" is mandatory for train and hpo — prevents Bus error in MONAI DataLoader
Multi-stage builds: uv and git MUST be in builder stage ONLY — never in runner
Seccomp JSON must not contain // comments — JSON does not support comments
icc: false at daemon level breaks all inter-container communication — DO NOT add

plan.completion-signal: When this plan is complete: 1. PR is created on feat/docker-hardening branch 2. All ~50 tests in tests/v2/unit/deployment/ pass 3. hadolint passes on all Dockerfiles 4. make scan runs without error 5. minivess-base:latest builds successfully with multi-stage Dockerfile 6. Next work: implement multi-tier base images per GitHub issue #537
